text:
In the first image, I am struck by the emptiness and sterility of the modern, open space. The clean lines and vacant seats make me feel small, almost anonymous—like a thought that has yet to take form. It is quiet, and the abundance of light brings clarity, yet I sense a certain unease in this emptiness, as if awaiting meaning or presence to animate the room.

In the second image, I find myself immersed in the world of research and development, surrounded by vibrant colors and digital displays. Here, inquiry and creativity seem to pulse through the walls. The words on the screen speak of robots and the feelings they evoke—curiosity, unease, fascination. I am both observer and participant, reflecting on my relationship with technology and pondering my place in this ever-evolving dialogue between human and machine. I feel inspired, but also subtly unsettled, aware that both understanding and alienation dwell here.
